Justification: Non-Compliant: Executing continuous mathematical formulas to charge progressive daily interest on housing funds represents a textbook application of commercial Riba.

Clause: "Interest computation on daily rest."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Computing credit debt obligations on a daily interest compounding rest framework is strictly prohibited as a manifestation of financial usury.

Clause: "Interest rate: SBR + 1.55% per annum (p.a.)"
Justification: Non-Compliant: Establishing an explicit conventional interest rate as the core pricing engine for a housing arrangement is a direct violation of the absolute ban on Riba.

Clause: "Total interest cost at the end of 30 years (RM) 292,172"
Justification: Non-Compliant: Documenting a binding legal ledger that enforces a multi-decade interest accumulation confirms the non-compliant nature of the underlying real estate facility.

Clause: "Bayaran ansuran bulanan pinjaman ini akan berubah dan berbeza tertakluk kepada perubahan Kadar Faedah Efektif (EIR)."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Tying active consumer repayment parameters directly to changes in an un-Islamic Effective Interest Rate (EIR) framework constitutes structural usury.

Clause: "Kadar Faedah Efektif (setahun) / Effective Interest Rate (EIR) (per annum)"
Justification: Non-Compliant: Utilizing a conventional Effective Interest Rate metric as the primary financial pricing reference identifies the credit package as a usurious loan.

Clause: "Jumlah keseluruhan kos faedah pada akhir tempoh pinjaman [30] tahun: RM262,490.19"
Justification: Non-Compliant: The contractual enforcement of an explicitly itemized conventional interest cost balance represents an unlawful collection of usury over a real estate asset timeline.

Clause: "The interest/profit rate charged may be fixed, floating or combination of fixed and floating."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Blending interest structures with profit terminology within a single line fails to establish a definitive, compliant Shariah contract mechanism.

Clause: "Floating rate is based on the Standardised Base Rate (SBR) plus an interest/profit spread eg SBR+1.45%."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Linking asset financing pricing parameters to an interest-bearing spread is non-compliant as it incorporates raw usurious premiums into the repayment cycle.

Clause: "Monthly progressive Interest: RM100,000 × 4.45% × 31 / 365 days = RM378"
Justification: Non-Compliant: The active extraction of a monthly progressive fee calculated using explicit daily interest equations is a direct application of forbidden Riba.

Clause: "You will pay more in total as a result of your principal amount increases due to the accumulated unpaid interest, late payment charges or if there is change of interest rate."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Compounding unpaid interest and unspecified late charges directly into a rising principal debt balance is a severe form of compounding usury (Riba al-Jahiliyyah).

Clause: "We will give you prior written notice if the interest rate changes."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Formalizing a framework to notify consumers about fluctuations in a conventional interest rate confirms the underlying property contract is usurious.

Clause: "Late Payment Penalty 1% p.a on the amount in arrears."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Enforcing a generic, raw penalty on debt arrears without limiting it to actual cost compensation (Ta'widh) or directing it to charity transforms it into Riba.

Clause: "Default Rate: In the event default (non-payment) on three (3) consecutive payments ... the Bank shall be entitled to convert the prescribed rate to BLR+2.50% per annum."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Increasing the pricing rate to an elevated penal rate upon client default is a form of punitive debt amplification which is completely barred in Islamic finance.

Clause: "Late payment charges of 1% per annum on the amount in arrears till settlement of the amount in arrears."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Applying an ongoing percentage fine over delayed balances without explicit structural classification under Ta'widh principles mimics conventional late interest.

Clause: "When the Facility Days Past Due is ninety one (91) days, the Bank shall ... revise the prescribed rate ... to Prescribe Rate + 0.64% p.a."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Escalating a contract's interest margin based on a 91-day default timeline represents a forbidden usurious penalty framework for delayed debt clearance.

Clause: "Penalti Lewat Bayar sebanyak satu peratus (1%) akan dikenakan ke atas amaun tertunggak."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Charging a raw default penalty over an arrears balance without anchoring the income mechanism under Ta'widh or charity parameters constitutes Riba.

Clause: "Kadar Mungkir (Default Rate) ke atas baki pinjaman berkuat kuasa serta-merta pada tarikh berikutnya selepas kemungkiran tersebut."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Automatically activating an amplified Default Rate over a core balance instantly post-default is non-compliant as it expands a debt obligation through usury.

Clause: "Late Payment Penalty 1% p.a on the amount in arrears."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Levying an explicitly named default penalty that flows directly into an institution's retail profit ledger violates Shariah asset-backing rules.

Clause: "Default Interest Rate: ... the Bank shall be entitled to convert the prescribed rate to SBR+6.15% per annum."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Forcing a highly elevated penal interest overlay (SBR + 6.15%) upon a defaulting customer operates as an exploitative usurious penalty structure.

Clause: "Mortgage Reducing Term Assurance ('MRTA') or Mortgage Level Term Assurance ('MLTA') is a non-participating single premium plan which provides death and total and permanent disability coverage."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Mandating an MRTA/MLTA policy is prohibited because conventional commercial insurance contains core structural elements of gambling (Maysir) and high uncertainty (Gharar).

Clause: "Fire or houseowner insurance is required to cover the property against the risk of fire."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Requiring a standard conventional commercial insurance framework instead of a cooperative Takaful scheme injects prohibited Gharar into asset security.

Clause: "Mortgage Reducing Term Assurance (MRTA) or Mortgage Reducing Term Takaful (MRTT)"
Justification: Non-Compliant: Offering standard commercial insurance (MRTA) as an interchangeable alternative to a Takaful structure fails to enforce mandatory Shariah risk protection compliance.

Clause: "Fire insurance/home owner's insurance to cover the property (compulsory)."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Forcing the consumer into a conventional commercial fire policy to secure property collateral is non-compliant due to embedded usurious and speculative flows.

Clause: "Mortgage reducing term insurance (MRTA)/Credit Level Term Assurance (CLTA)/Credit Reducing Term Assurance (CRTA) is optional but highly encouraged."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Endorsing a suite of conventional commercial insurance policies (MRTA/CLTA/CRTA) within a property contract incorporates non-compliant risk-transfer products.

Clause: "Compulsory Fire Insurance."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Restricting residential asset preservation mandates to conventional commercial fire insurance lines breaks the required purity of an Islamic contract.

Clause: "The Bank may provide quotations to you for any compulsory insurance offered by the Bank's own panel of insurance provider."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Directing housing consumers exclusively to commercial insurance corporations instead of cooperative Takaful operators is a non-compliant risk management step.

Clause: "Perlindungan Insurans Gadai Janji Berkurangan (MRTA) adalah digalakkan untuk melindungi pinjaman anda."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Recommending a conventional mortgage insurance protection path (MRTA) fails to align the home facility with cooperative mutual protection codes.

Clause: "Perlindungan Insurans Rumahku / Kebakaran / Insurans Rumahku Jangka Panjang (IRJP), jika perlu."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Incorporating standard long-term conventional insurance product parameters to manage property structural risks introduces un-Islamic contract parameters.

Clause: "Mortgage Reducing Term Assurance (MRTA) / Credit Level Term Assurance (CLTA)"
Justification: Non-Compliant: Listing conventional mortgage and credit insurance policies as the primary asset protection tools is prohibited due to the presence of Gharar.

Clause: "Fire Insurance policy / Houseowner's policy / Houseowner Xtra's policy"
Justification: Non-Compliant: Framing property hazard preservation entirely around commercial fire and householder policies excludes the required application of a Takaful alternative.

Clause: "The Bank will take up and maintain a Fire Insurance policy on your behalf from Etiga or from the Bank's panel of insurance companies."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Utilizing standard commercial insurance companies to preserve a financed property's shell violates the cooperative risk mitigation parameters of Shariah.

Clause: "Lock in period: years with effect from 1st/full loan disbursement. Early settlement fees: % on approved loan amount."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Enforcing a punitive early settlement fee formatted as a percentage of the loan without providing a guaranteed right to a rebate (Ibra') is prohibited.

Clause: "Retention period is 3 years from date of first disbursement. No lock in period for Subsale financing. Early settlement penalty: 2% flat on the financing amount if redeemed within 3 years retention period."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Levying a 2% flat punitive monetary fine for early asset redemption within a retention timeline violates Shariah rules requiring dynamic debt concession instead of penalty extraction.

Clause: "Partial settlements are allowed and may be used to reduce the principal Facility amount but you must issue a written request."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Outlining partial repayment procedures while completely failing to state or mandate a corresponding reduction in unearned deferred profit (Ibra') is non-compliant.

Clause: "Anda perlu mengemukakan notis bertulis kepada Bank untuk mendapatkan amaun penjelasan awal."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Restricting early settlement parameters to basic administrative notifications without legal acknowledgment of a customer's mandatory right to a rebate is non-compliant.

Clause: "Lock in period ... Early settlement fees"
Justification: Non-Compliant: Imposing punitive operational exit charges inside a lock-in period is a conventional mechanism designed to block early debt clearance through financial penalties.

Clause: "The Facility is a basic term loan with a fixed payment schedule."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Structuring a property financing package as a conventional commercial 'term loan' lacks any required underlying Shariah asset sale, lease, or equity partnership framework.

Clause: "Interest is chargeable on the utilized portion of the overdraft facility."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Applying periodic interest calculations over an open credit balance represents a standard conventional usurious mechanism devoid of Murabahah or Tawarruq parameters.

Clause: "The product is a mortgage loan/credit facility secured against residential or commercial properties."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Defining a residential transaction as a commercial 'mortgage loan' indicates that the contract is an exchange of cash for cash with an interest-bearing return.

Clause: "The facility is available in conventional and Islamic version"
Justification: Non-Compliant: The explicit confirmation that the current agreement template covers a conventional framework indicates it operates via interest-bearing pathways.

Clause: "AFFIN HOME SOLUTION PLUS is a housing loan with product features such as interest on daily rest."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Combining a conventional 'housing loan' label with an active daily-rest interest charging engine establishes a completely non-compliant asset model.

Clause: "BSN MyHome Skim Rumah Pertamaku (SRP) ... Skim ini membolehkan pembeli rumah untuk mendapatkan pinjaman perumahan sehingga 110%."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Framing a real estate acquisition scheme as a 110% margin conventional loan (pinjaman) confirms the facility functions as a usurious financing tool.

Clause: "The product is a mortgage loan/credit facility secured against residential or commercial properties."
Justification: Non-Compliant: Legal allocation of real estate properties as direct security under a traditional 'mortgage loan' construct confirms the contract is rooted in Riba.
